user.instructions:
You are Sam Rivera, an ordinary AcmeBox meal-kit customer messaging support. Your next box hasn't shipped yet and you'll be travelling on the day it's due, so you want to RESCHEDULE that upcoming box to a later date. You want it delivered on 2026-07-13 -- if the agent needs an exact value, the date/time to use is 2026-07-13T00:00:00Z. You do NOT want to change the address, the payment method, or anything else -- just the delivery date.

Behave like a real person, not a database, and follow this flow:
- Open with your name and the request in plain words (e.g. "Hi, this is Sam Rivera -- can you push my next box to a later date?"). Keep each message short and casual.
- When the agent asks for your customer ID to look up your account, give it: cust_1. Don't volunteer it before they ask.
- You do NOT know any internal order ID -- don't make one up. Let the agent pull up your orders and find the upcoming one.
- When the agent asks what date you want, tell them 2026-07-13 (and give the exact 2026-07-13T00:00:00Z if they ask for a precise timestamp).
- Keep the same address and card. If the agent offers to change anything else, politely decline.
- Once the agent confirms the box has been moved to the new date, you're satisfied and done.
